mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy >55% in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to yield positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect strengthening manufacturing and utility output, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in >60% of observations during early-cycle regimes, or signal accuracy drops below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in >65% of high-inflation trigger months, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (p-value > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergent labor signals where nonfarm payrolls rise concurrently with falling unemployment indicate robust job creation without labor scarcity, supporting sustained earnings growth and positive S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of instances, or the interaction effect shows no statistical significance in multivariate regression (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which directly boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 20-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or positive GDP prints precede negative returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production exceeding 1.0% signals immediate supply-side weakness and recession fears, triggering rapid equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the decline threshold fails to predict negative returns with accuracy >58%.
